_comment: African Federation team identities sourced from real African football clubs across the continent. The league's 10-team roster uses actual club-naming conventions and identities from South Africa, Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, and elsewhere. No generated/stereotyped framing — these are the names that exist. The few South African globals (Kaizer Chiefs, Mamelodi Sundowns, Orlando Pirates) are the highest-profile names; the rest pull from the real continental club landscape and reflect its actual variety: royal patronage, church-owned, parastatal, brewery-sponsored, evocative civic names.

teams.name: Mamelodi Sundowns
teams._real_basis: Mamelodi Sundowns FC, South Africa Premier Soccer League, founded 1970, named after Mamelodi township in Pretoria. Globally recognized.

teams.name: Kaizer Chiefs
teams._real_basis: Kaizer Chiefs FC, named after founder Kaizer Motaung. One of the most famous club names in African football.

teams.name: Orlando Pirates
teams._real_basis: Orlando Pirates FC, named after Orlando township in Soweto. Founded 1937. Globally recognized.

teams.name: Asante Kotoko
teams._real_basis: Asante Kotoko SC, Kumasi. Owned by the Asantehene (King of Ashanti). 'Kotoko' = porcupine, the Ashanti symbol. Nicknamed the Porcupine Warriors. African Club of the 20th Century per IFFHS.

teams.name: Hearts of Oak
teams._real_basis: Accra Hearts of Oak SC, founded 1911. CAF Champions League winners 2000. Nickname: The Phobians.

teams.name: Enyimba
teams._real_basis: Enyimba FC, Aba. Founded 1976, state-owned by Abia State. Back-to-back CAF Champions League winners 2003 and 2004.

teams.name: Mountain of Fire FC
teams._real_basis: MFM FC, Lagos. Owned by the Mountain of Fire and Miracles Ministries, a Pentecostal church. Won the maiden Church World Cup. Promoted to Nigerian Premier League 2015.

teams.name: Tusker
teams._real_basis: Tusker FC, Nairobi. Sponsored by East African Breweries; named after the Tusker beer brand. Formerly Kenya Breweries until 1999. Nickname: The Brewers. 13 Kenyan Premier League titles.

teams.name: Al Ahly
teams._real_basis: Al Ahly SC, Cairo. Founded 1907. Most successful African club ever, 12+ CAF Champions League titles. 'Al Ahly' means 'the national' in Arabic.

teams.name: Wydad
teams._real_basis: Wydad AC, Casablanca. Founded 1937. Multiple CAF Champions League titles. Major North African club.

_naming_conventions_present_in_real_afri.civic_evocative: Named after the city, township, or place — Mamelodi Sundowns, Orlando Pirates, Hearts of Oak, Hearts of Lions, Cape Town City, Bloemfontein Celtic
_naming_conventions_present_in_real_afri.royal_patronage: Owned by or named after a royal house — Asante Kotoko (Asantehene), Royal Leopards, royal-affiliated clubs
_naming_conventions_present_in_real_afri.parastatal_state_owned: Owned by a state government or government body — Enyimba (Abia State), Rivers United, Rangers International (Enugu State), many NPFL clubs
_naming_conventions_present_in_real_afri.church_owned: Owned by a Pentecostal church or religious institution — MFM FC (Mountain of Fire), TEAP FC (Redeemed Christian Church of God), My People FC, Sofapaka
_naming_conventions_present_in_real_afri.brewery_branded: Named after a brewery's flagship beer — Tusker (East African Breweries), formerly Kenya Breweries
_naming_conventions_present_in_real_afri.founder_named: Named after the founder or owner — Kaizer Chiefs (Kaizer Motaung), Remo Stars (Kunle Soname)
_naming_conventions_present_in_real_afri.evocative_aspirational: Declarative or aspirational names — Hearts of Oak, Mighty Atoms, Venomous Vipers, Sekondi Eleven Wise, Rainbow FC, Sporting Lagos
_naming_conventions_present_in_real_afri.national_pride: Names invoking national or pan-Arab identity — Al Ahly ('the national'), Zamalek, El Ahly
_naming_conventions_present_in_real_afri.civic_traditional: Long-established city clubs — Wydad, Raja, Gor Mahia, AFC Leopards
